title: Retrospective change record for segmentation and outcome-consistency gate
summary: The selected proof-case intervention is now tied to concrete live code and git commits. The record is retrospective: the gate existed before the proof case was formalized, but it is now logged explicitly as the active change surface for Step 4.
change_scope: selection-layer guard for regression candidate emission

change_surface.role: encodes task_type gate and outcome-definition consistency gate
applies the gate while selecting regression candidates from state.db

foundation_commit.subject: feat: add bounded-autonomy telemetry helpers and signal-router tooling

activation_commit.subject: fix: centralize session finalize eval and skill tracking

activation_reference.note: Best grounded activation point for the conservative gate shape used in the proof case.

implementation_notes: The proof-case change record is retrospective rather than newly executed in this session.
The change was not introduced solely for this proof case; the proof case is now explicitly anchored to the existing live implementation.
The earlier 2026-04-13 commit provides the draft-generation foundation, while the 2026-04-19 commit is the best grounded activation point for the current gate behavior.

affected_metrics_and_views.name: regression candidate emission
affected_metrics_and_views.effect: Candidates with missing task_type or outcome-definition inconsistency are suppressed before draft generation.

affected_metrics_and_views.name: skill-level regression handling posture
affected_metrics_and_views.effect: Null-segmented or internally inconsistent cases are treated as instrumentation-first rather than direct skill evidence.

affected_metrics_and_views.name: downstream review workload quality
affected_metrics_and_views.effect: Expected reduction in false-positive regression candidates reaching review.

rollback.strategy: Revert or remove the gate definition/invocation and restore prior regression candidate emission behavior.

verification_basis: Live files contain selection_gate_reason() and regression-candidate suppression logic.
Git history grounds the current gate behavior to concrete commits on 2026-04-13 and 2026-04-19.
The selected proof-case intervention matches the live implementation surface.
limitations: This is a retrospective record, not a freshly executed code change in the proof-case session.
The gate existed before the proof case was formalized, so causality toward later outcomes still requires a bounded measurement window.
Historical provenance gaps in legacy rows are still unresolved.

next_required_step.name: Measure bounded post-change effect
actions: define a baseline comparator for pre/post candidate quality
set the observation window and minimum threshold
record whether false-positive regression candidates actually drop
